rationale: Both candidates converge on the same core diagnosis (test hits the intentional NotImplementedError guard added by #4834 when sleep(level=2) is followed by wake_up(), which matches the traceback in the issue body and yenuo26's comment attributing the failure to #4834), and both recommend switching to level=1 — but the actual thread shows this was still under triage (Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI run), not a confirmed closed/fixed state, so both overclaim resolution confidence. Y is slightly better grounded because it explicitly states it checked the live checkout and found level=1 already present, whereas X asserts 'already applied on main' plus several additional test names/line ranges without stating how it verified them, and pads with runtime production guidance that's tangential given the reporter's environment is stated as 'CI env' (a merge-CI failure, not a live user issue).
